description: Quadratic vs linear fit comparison for A_FB angular distribution
conclusion: The primary extraction uses |cos(theta)| bins where the linear fit gives chi2/ndf = 10.9/8 (p = 0.208), which is acceptable. A quadratic term improves the fit marginally (F-test p = 0.084) with a cos^2 coefficient c = -0.0300 +/- 0.0150. The A_FB extracted from the linear coefficient is stable: linear gives 0.1482, quadratic gives 0.3026. In the signed cos(theta) extraction, the cos^2 term is highly significant (F-test p = 0.0109), reflecting a symmetric purity/acceptance effect that creates the parabolic shape. The |cos(theta)| extraction correctly isolates the asymmetry signal.

signed_cos.note: The cos^2 term is a symmetric purity/acceptance effect. In signed cos(theta), it creates the parabolic shape. In |cos(theta)|, it adds a smooth bias but the linear slope correctly captures the asymmetry.
